evaluation_protocol.topic: PPO stabilization and Edge-Gated Actor-Critic ablation
evaluation_protocol.integrity_note: This file is an evaluation protocol and report-planning template. Numeric result fields are intentionally left blank until measured from actual runs. Do not present this file as completed experimental evidence without replacing placeholders with real measurements.
evaluation_protocol.research_question: Can lightweight self-play stabilization methods and a boundary-aware actor architecture reduce brittle behavior in a Unity-based sumo self-play environment?

method.name: Plain PPO
method.description: Standard actor-critic PPO without two-policy alternation, opponent pool, mutation, EMA, or EGAC.
method.purpose: Baseline for measuring the difficulty of the Unity arena task.

method.name: Alternating PPO + Reward/Observation Shaping
method.description: Two-policy alternating self-play with 17-dimensional observations, edge-aware reward shaping, and left-right canonicalization.
method.purpose: Measures the effect of the core self-play training setup.

method.name: Enhanced Self-Play
method.description: Alternating self-play with opponent-pool sampling, Gaussian checkpoint mutation, and EMA propagation.
method.purpose: Measures whether diversity and softer A/B policy transfer improve robustness.

method.name: Enhanced Self-Play + EGAC
method.description: Enhanced self-play plus Edge-Gated Actor-Critic. The edge gate uses boundary features to damp outward movement and reduce push probability near the ring boundary.
method.purpose: Tests the project-specific structural contribution for reducing boundary-risk failures.

metric.definition: Average undiscounted episodic return over evaluation episodes.
metric.interpretation: Higher is generally better, but should be read together with win rate and timeout rate.

metric.definition: Fraction of evaluation episodes won against scripted heuristic opponents.
metric.interpretation: Measures basic policy competence outside latest-vs-latest self-play.

metric.definition: Fraction of evaluation episodes won against earlier saved checkpoints.
metric.interpretation: Measures robustness against a broader opponent distribution.

metric.definition: Fraction of episodes lost by the evaluated agent leaving the arena boundary.
metric.interpretation: Primary safety metric for EGAC. Lower is better.

metric.definition: Fraction of push actions triggered while the agent edge margin is below the chosen near-edge threshold.
metric.interpretation: Diagnostic metric for whether EGAC reduces high-risk push behavior near the boundary.

metric.definition: Fraction of episodes ending by time limit.
metric.interpretation: Checks whether safety improvements come from excessive passivity.

metric.definition: Average number of environment steps before terminal state.
metric.interpretation: Used to distinguish immediate failures, stable engagement, and passive stalling.

recommended_evaluation.notes: If time is limited, prioritize self_ring_out_rate, win_rate_vs_heuristic, timeout_rate, and one qualitative Unity video comparison.

result.qualitative_observation: Expected failure mode to check: unstable movement, overuse of push, and frequent accidental ring-outs.
Expected trend to check: more stable than plain PPO, but still vulnerable near the boundary.
Expected trend to check: better opponent robustness from pool sampling and mutation, with possible remaining boundary-risk mistakes.
Expected trend to check: fewer self ring-outs and fewer near-edge pushes, while preserving center-area engagement.

report_language_template.safe_claim: We implemented the EGAC mechanism and designed an ablation protocol comparing plain PPO, alternating self-play, enhanced self-play, and EGAC. Due to time constraints, the final report emphasizes implementation, qualitative behavior, and the planned evaluation metrics rather than claiming exhaustive benchmark results.
report_language_template.claim_after_measurement: Replace this text only after running evaluation: EGAC changed self-ring-out rate from [baseline_value] to [egac_value] over [n] episodes, suggesting that the boundary-aware gate reduced high-risk actions near the arena edge.
